key_insight: An interpretable vision transformer can classify fracture causes in zirconia-toughened alumina from SEM images, and low-magnification views can be nearly as informative as high-magnification ones.
engineering_relevance: This enables faster, less subjective implant fracture QA by using low-magnification SEM as an effective pre-screening step for ceramic quality control.
method: Fine-tuned ViT on 8,493 SEM images spanning 50x to 10,000x magnification.
Classified fractures into three manufacturing-related defect classes: green body, hard machining, and material defects.
Used Grad-CAM plus leakage auditing to check interpretability and specimen independence.
evidence_signal: In stratified five-fold cross-validation, the model achieved 0.907 accuracy and 0.888 macro-F1, with comparable performance at 50x and at 1k-10kx magnification.
watch_item: The workflow was validated on a specific ceramic implant material and manufacturing test dataset, so broader deployment will need external validation across other scanners, labs, and failure modes.
paper_title: Low-Magnification SEM May Suffice: Interpretable Deep Learning for Multi-Scale Fracture-Cause Classification in Zirconia-Toughened Alumina
